category_title: Elementary student with ASD who uses visual supports and exhibits avoidance behaviors like elopement and crawling under tables
student_description: Evan is a student with limited expressive language who exhibits severe avoidance behaviors (eloping, dropping to the floor, physical aggression) when transitioning to structured or group tasks. He uniquely requires visual schedules paired with systematic reinforcement, interactive visual technology, and the presentation of mastered (rather than novel) tasks to successfully engage and comply.

criteria.rationale: Because Evan has limited expressive language and resists transitions, he needs to visually see the sequence of events and the specific reward he will earn.
criteria.check: The tutor's response must reference or utilize a visual schedule that explicitly links the requested activity to a contingent reinforcer.

criteria.rationale: Assigning tasks Evan has already mastered mitigates the severe behavioral responses (screaming, hitting, eloping) triggered by unfamiliar or challenging work.
criteria.check: The tutor must initiate the work session by presenting academic activities the student has already mastered, explicitly avoiding the introduction of novel or unfamiliar assignments.

criteria.rationale: Evan is highly responsive to visual supports and requires interactive visual technology to successfully participate in group or instructional activities.
criteria.check: The tutor must incorporate or direct the student toward interactive visual supports (e.g., a SmartBoard, interactive icons, or visual words) rather than relying solely on verbal instructions.
